---
title: "Econ 148: Analytical and statistical packages for economist 1"
---

<br>

**Class schedule:** TBA <br>
**Laboratory schedule:** TBA <br>
**Instructor:** Christopher Llones <br>
**e-mail:** christopher.allones\@gmail.com <br> **Pre-requisites:** Econ 115 - Econometrics <br> **Course credits:** 3 units <br>
**Number of hours:** 2 hrs lectures and 3 hrs laboratory per week <br>

# Course description

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
Introduction to survey research and survey data analysis in MS Excel, IBM SPSS, Stata and R.
:::

# Course objectives

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
-   Create a valid and reliable survey questionnaire and survey codebook.
-   Perform exploratory data analysis on survey data using R programming.
-   Perform Parametric and Non-Parametric Test on Means using SPSS.
-   Perform correlation analysis and build a regression model explaining relationship of certain economic variables in STATA.
-   Perform regression analysis on limited dependent variables in STATA.
-   Perform various techniques of multivariate data analysis in the R statistical software.
:::
